---
title: "EIT"
subtitle: "electromagnetically induced transparency"
author: "Awais Ansari & Dominic Nieder"
format:
  revealjs: 
    chalkboard: true
    css: style.css
    scroll-view:
      layout: compact
      snap: proximity
      progress: true
      activation-width: 0
    html-math-method: katex
    slide-number: true
---
## {background-image="EIT-summmary.jpg"}

## Rubidium

- $^{87}\text{Rb}\ (I=3/2)$ & $^{85}\text{Rb}\ (I=5/2)$
- Fine splitting (L-S coupling): $J = |L-S|, \ldots, L+S$
- Hyperfine splitting (I-J coupling): $F = |I-J|, \ldots, I+J$
- Zeeman effect: $\Delta E = g_F \mu_B m_F B_\text{ext}$

## Rubidium


## Extenal cavity diode laser

- internal cavity: semi-conductor / p-n-junction -> (x, y, z, I, T)

- "feedback system": diffraction grating on a mirror

- external cavity: reflection of difrationgrating into the diode (large L -> small spectral width)

<<<<<<< HEAD
<<<<<<< HEAD
=======
>>>>>>> 8c12701 (preparing: lab_notes. literature collection)



## Fabry-Pérot interferometer



<<<<<<< HEAD
=======
## Fabry-Pérot interferometer

>>>>>>> 325ba2e (presentation)
=======
>>>>>>> 8c12701 (preparing: lab_notes. literature collection)
## spectroscopic techniques

- flourescence spectroscopy
- absorbtion spectroscopy
- saturation spectroscopy

## atomic spectrum broadening

-
